title: Pelargonium × sulphureum
intro: Pelargonium × sulphureum, also known as yellow rose geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and pungent scent. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in soothing digestive issues, relieving stress and anxiety, and promoting a restful sleep. The bioactive constituents of Pelargonium × sulphureum include geraniol, linalool, and citronellol, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pelargonium × sulphureum is a member of the family Geraniaceae. It belongs to the genus Pelargonium and is a hybrid species. The hybrid was created by crossing two other species within the same genus. This classification places it alongside other related plants such as Pelargonium peltatum and Pelargonium tomentosum. The exact species it was crossed with is not widely documented.
uses: Pelargonium × sulphureum is known for its traditional medicinal uses in Southern Africa, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. It has been used to ease digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its astringent properties. Additionally, the plant has been utilized to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The roots of the plant have also been used as a natural remedy for fever and other infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal applications and potential benefits.
benefits: Pelargonium × sulphureum, a type of geranium plant, is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The leaves and flowers of the plant are used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to promote healing and relaxation. Some studies suggest that Pelargonium × sulphureum may have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential therapeutic applications.
properties: Pelargonium × sulphureum is a species of flowering plant in the geranium family, and like many other plants in the same family, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. parts: Pelargonium × sulphureum is commonly known as the yellow-leafed or yellow-leaved Geranium.  The leaves of Pelargonium × sulphureum are the medicinal parts of the plant, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. They are also said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves are typically harvested, dried, and then used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture.
